summary: In this brief, humorous post, Cantrill responds to being compared to infomercial pitchman Ron Popeil by The Register. He playfully 'clarifies' that DTrace cannot make turkey-jerky and denies rumors of a DTrace infomercial, while jokingly playing along with the promotional comparison.
key_points: The Register compared Cantrill's DTrace evangelism to Ron Popeil's pitchman style
Cantrill responds with self-deprecating humor rather than defensiveness
The post demonstrates Cantrill's willingness to engage with media characterizations playfully
money_quotes: Despite some claims to the contrary, DTrace cannot be used to make turkey-jerky.
It is true that many have used DTrace to work their way up from lowly busboys to yacht-owning multi-millionaires…
key_insight: Cantrill embraces rather than resists being characterized as an enthusiastic promoter of DTrace, using humor to acknowledge his passionate advocacy for the technology.
